race.name: 3RIDES Gran Fondo Südliche Weinstrasse
race.tagline: UCI Gran Fondo qualifier through German vineyards and into France

vitals.location: Porte des Vins Allemands (German Wine Gate), Schweigen-Rechtenbach, Rhineland-Palatinate, Germany

climate.overview: April in Rhineland-Palatinate features mild spring weather suitable for outdoor cycling, though past events faced thunderstorm risks.
climate.precipitation_chance: Moderate, with historical cancellations due to unstable weather

terrain.overview: Rolling hills through Pfalz vineyards, forests, and a 15 km cross-border section into France; completely car-free and technically demanding.

logistics.explanation: Accessible wine region near borders, but airports and costs unspecified.

length.explanation: Solid 100 km distance, corrected from incorrect 150 km claims.

technicality.explanation: Technically demanding with varied terrain through vineyards and forests.

elevation.explanation: Over 600 m provides challenge without extremes.

climate.explanation: Spring conditions variable; past weather issues noted.

altitude.explanation: Low elevation in hilly wine country.

adventure.explanation: Cross-border into France adds unique thrill.

prestige.explanation: UCI qualifier status with top 25% qualification.

race_quality.explanation: Part of established 3RIDES series with high production.

experience.explanation: Scenic wine route and car-free course enhance enjoyment.

community.explanation: Open to amateurs 19+ with age groups; field size unknown.

field_depth.explanation: Amateur-focused with potential pros from UCI status.

value.explanation: Qualifier value high, but registration costs unclear.

cultural_impact.explanation: Tied to iconic German Wine Route and border culture.

biased_opinion.overview: This UCI qualifier delivers a picturesque yet punchy ride through wine country, with the France border jaunt as a standout. Corrects user myths on distance/elevation; solid T2 road event for Euro spring training.

final_verdict.one_liner: Technically demanding UCI road qualifier in scenic Pfalz wine lands.
final_verdict.full_verdict: Relocated 100 km loop offers car-free challenge, cross-border adventure, and qualification path in a storied wine region; ideal for amateurs seeking prestige without gravel extremes.

logistics.overview: Start/finish at landmark German Wine Gate near French border; previously in Aachen.
logistics.accommodation: Local wineries, hotels like Land-gut-Hotel Schweigener Hof, and guesthouses abundant.
logistics.registration_notes: Open now for licensed amateurs 19+; costs unspecified; age-group categories.

history.overview: Gran Fondo debut 2024 in Aachen; 2026 edition at Schweigen-Rechtenbach as 3RIDES Südliche Weinstrasse, evolved from 2023 gravel event.
notable_moments: Marianne Vos won 2024 women's race in Aachen.
Prior Gran Fondo canceled due to thunderstorms.

course_description.character: Rolling 100 km loop: vineyards, forests, gentle hills; 15 km into France; technically challenging, car-free.
course_description.signature_challenge: 15 km French border section through wine trails

non_negotiables: UCI Gran Fondo World Series qualifier
Top 25% per age group qualifies for Worlds

citations.title: UCI Gran Fondo World Series Page
